claim_scope: In a controlled local HTTP API serving cached open-weight GPT-2-family models, a normalized request/model-fingerprint/semantic-response/tool/state hash-chain replay ledger detected and localized all tested injected material drift while avoiding benign formatting false positives across 100 fixed-seed DistilGPT-2 traces.
scale_limits: The validation used GPT-2-family next-token completions, local HTTP serving, deterministic tool/state observations, and injected drift modes. It did not test live provider model changes, instruction-tuned chat models, streaming deltas, retries, rate limits, billing/API failures, long multi-tool agents, external side effects, or naturally occurring longitudinal drift.
useful_signal_summary: On the bounded DistilGPT-2 run, the full ledger achieved 500/500 material drift detection, 0/200 benign false positives, and 500/500 material localization. Output-only detected only 192/500 material cases, raw transcript detected 500/500 material cases but produced 100/200 benign false positives, and ablations lost the expected component-specific localization or detection signal.
recommended_next_action: Stop as no-paper useful signal; only a live or production-like longitudinal API study with streaming/retry/tool side effects should revisit paper readiness.
stop_reason: The result is direct bounded open-weight mechanism support, not publication-grade evidence, because all drift was controlled/injected and the task/model setting is too narrow.
